title: ctenitis subcrenulata
intro: Ctenitis subcrenulata, also known as the Ctenitis fern, is a species of fern in the family Dryopteridaceae. It is native to tropical and subtropical regions of Asia and has been traditionally used in medicine for its various health benefits. The medicinal properties of Ctenitis subcrenulata include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties, which contribute to its health benefits, such as reducing fever, relieving pain, and treating respiratory issues. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or extracts for use in traditional medicine, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety for modern medical applications.
